jokes.text: Shut up, bird.
jokes.setup: Character tries to contribute to conversation
jokes.punchline: Mac dismissively calls Dee a bird, cutting off her input
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character integration - this insult is uniquely Mac's way of shutting down Dee

jokes.text: These guys are blasting us in the ass.
jokes.setup: Discussion about unfair treatment by trash workers
jokes.punchline: Charlie uses crude sexual metaphor for being taken advantage of
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie malapropism - absurd sexual metaphor delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Who am I supposed to vote for, the Republican who's blasting me in the ass or the Democrat who's blasting me in the ass?
jokes.setup: Mac being called out for not voting
jokes.punchline: Mac doubles down on the ass-blasting metaphor for all of politics
jokes.explanation: Escalates Charlie's metaphor to encompass all politics - absurd but logical extension

jokes.text: Yeah, politics is all one big ass-blast.
jokes.punchline: Charlie simplifies it to one universal ass-blast
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - reduces complex political discourse to simple crude metaphor

jokes.text: Let's pull up our bootstraps, oil up a couple asses and do a little plowing of our own. Pow!
jokes.setup: Frank trying to motivate the group with inspiring language
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describes gay sex with enthusiastic 'Pow!'
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - starts as motivational speech, becomes accidental homoerotic imagery. The 'Pow!' sells it perfectly.

jokes.text: Not gay sex.
jokes.setup: Frank realizes what he just said
jokes.punchline: Awkward clarification that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that make the situation more awkward rather than fixing it

jokes.text: Okay, yeah. It seemed like... What are you talking about?
jokes.punchline: Confused reaction that prolongs the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good beat that extends the awkward moment, though not as strong as Frank's line.

jokes.text: Shut up, bird.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to point out they've done this before
jokes.punchline: Mac shuts her down with the bird insult again
jokes.explanation: Second use of the running gag - slightly less impact but still effective character moment

jokes.text: Meanwhile, I dress Dee up like a whore, get a city official, bribe him, and make him give us the contract
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his sophisticated business plan
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions prostitution and bribery as normal business tactics
jokes.explanation: Dark subversive humor - Frank treats illegal activities as mundane business strategy

jokes.text: We've gone to door-to-door in a van before, and that didn't work out for us.
jokes.punchline: Charlie references their history of failed schemes
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about the show's recurring premise of failed schemes

jokes.text: How often should it work out? Almost never.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking about their success rate
jokes.punchline: Charlie honestly admits they almost never succeed
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure exposing the futility of their schemes

jokes.text: And I'm still the wild card, so that's good.
jokes.punchline: Charlie proudly identifies as the chaotic element they're trying to eliminate
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Charlie obliviously celebrates being the problem they're trying to solve

jokes.text: I feel like you just agreed with me, but you weren't listening to what I was saying.
jokes.punchline: Dennis recognizes Charlie is agreeing without comprehension
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about miscommunication - Dennis's frustration with Charlie's obtuseness

jokes.text: I feel like you just agreed with me, but you weren't listening to what I was saying. Yes. You pointed at me like I said something, but I didn't. Good.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining Charlie doesn't benefit the group
jokes.punchline: Charlie's nonsensical responses proving Dennis's point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant recursive joke where Charlie demonstrates exactly what Dennis is criticizing. Meta and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Yeah, he doesn't even like, get us, man. It's... We're talking about you.
jokes.punchline: Mac has to explicitly tell Charlie they're insulting him to his face
jokes.explanation: Awkward revelation - Mac's frustration boils over into stating the obvious

jokes.text: Yeah, sure, in the 1950s, yeah, you could drive around door to door in a windowless van, and people didn't think they were gonna get raped. Now, th-they will think that.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Frank's van plan is outdated
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to rape fears with modern awareness
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about changing social awareness. Dennis's casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You're presenting yourself as a banana.
jokes.punchline: Frank's brutal assessment of her yellow outfit
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag - simple, specific, and perfectly captures Frank's bluntness

jokes.text: I did not picture this guy getting recycled back into the mix.
jokes.punchline: Uses 'recycled' as a pun given the trash-themed episode
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay connecting the trash theme to their recurring character problem

jokes.text: ♪ I still got the moves... ♪
jokes.setup: Frank trying to seduce the city official
jokes.punchline: Frank awkwardly singing while presumably dancing/stripping
jokes.explanation: Cringe physical comedy - Frank's delusional confidence about his seductive abilities

jokes.text: ♪ I still got the moves... ♪
jokes.setup: Frank trying to seduce the city official
jokes.punchline: Frank singing while presumably dancing/stripping
jokes.explanation: Frank's delusional confidence about his seduction skills. Visual humor that works in character.

jokes.text: Let's not do accents. That's the kind of wild-card stuff that always gets us into trouble.
jokes.punchline: Dennis trying to prevent their typical disasters
jokes.explanation: Great meta-reference to the show's pattern of accent-based disasters. Dennis as voice of reason is ironic.

jokes.text: Mac, you have an exceptional number of bugs in your teeth.
jokes.setup: Mac riding on the back of the limo
jokes.punchline: Dennis's disgusted observation about the consequences
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag with logical setup - riding with mouth open leads to bug consumption

jokes.text: ♪ Hello, We bet you hate that smell, That smell from all your trash ♪
jokes.punchline: Singing a song about how much someone's trash stinks
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd sales strategy - insulting potential customers in song form

jokes.text: Wait, I have to stop you. Damn it, Dennis. I knew this wasn't gonna work. No, the plan was good. Charlie, you were sharp. I was... You were a little sharp.
jokes.punchline: Customer actually wants their service, critique is musical
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - we expect rejection but get acceptance with musical critique. Well-structured surprise.

jokes.text: The elaborate door-to-door sales song about trash collection
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top musical number for garbage collection services
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment with strong commitment to the bit, though slightly long

jokes.text: My hunch is that this gentleman has gone from a twink to a twunk to a twank.
jokes.setup: Mac diagnosing the official's sexual orientation
jokes.punchline: Mac's encyclopedic knowledge of gay male subcategories
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Mac's suspiciously detailed knowledge of gay culture while claiming to be straight. Creative wordplay with 'twank'.

jokes.text: What's a twunk? Twink and a hunk. A twink with muscles, but still hairless. So smooth. Incredibly smooth.
jokes.punchline: Mac's detailed explanation with emphasis on smoothness
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the gay taxonomy. Mac's enthusiasm for 'smoothness' adds another layer of humor.

jokes.text: Twank is no good. That's a twink and a skank. Essentially a ragdoll, just being passed around from twink to twunk to bear to otter.
jokes.punchline: Mac's judgmental description of 'twank' lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Further escalation with Mac showing surprising knowledge and judgment about gay culture.

jokes.text: the otter generates his power through extraordinary quickness, cunning, and skill.
jokes.punchline: Describing gay men like Pokémon with special abilities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation treating human sexuality like nature documentary - Mac's absurd expertise continues

jokes.text: Some cocks can't be unsucked.
jokes.setup: Discussion about sexual orientation being permanent
jokes.punchline: Frank's crude but philosophically profound statement
jokes.explanation: Near-perfect joke - vulgar but unexpectedly profound, perfect economy, quintessential Frank wisdom. Subverts expectation of crude throwaway into meaningful statement.

jokes.text: I, for one, am of the belief that some things can't be thrown in the trash, Frank. And your sexuality is one of them.
jokes.punchline: Mac's philosophy tied to episode's trash theme
jokes.explanation: Clever thematic connection between sexuality and trash theme. Mac being unexpectedly philosophical.

jokes.text: I forgot. / Well, I guess we'll be the ones to slink away.
jokes.setup: Frank realizing he's still pointing a gun
jokes.punchline: The understated acknowledgment that they should leave
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment with nice economy

jokes.text: The gulls are shitting all over me.
jokes.setup: Mac riding on back of limo filled with trash
jokes.punchline: Natural consequence - seagulls attracted to garbage
jokes.explanation: Logical visual gag - trash attracts birds, birds poop on Mac. Simple cause and effect humor.

jokes.text: Let's just dump it in a poor neighborhood because they don't mind living in their own filth. Absolutely. They don't know any better. I mean, why else would they choose to live like that? They don't care? They clearly don't care.
jokes.setup: Looking for easier solution than driving to New Jersey
jokes.punchline: Casual classist logic escalating through agreement
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about class privilege. The escalation through multiple characters makes their awfulness clear.

jokes.text: let's just dump it in a poor neighborhood because they don't mind living in their own filth.
jokes.setup: Looking for easy solution to trash disposal
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual classist assumption about poor people
jokes.explanation: Dark character comedy - Dennis's privileged worldview casually dismisses poor people as subhuman

jokes.text: He just tried to kiss me. / Yeah, he did a little bit.
jokes.punchline: The casual confirmation with 'a little bit'
jokes.explanation: Simple understatement with good economy

jokes.text: He's trying to confuse us with his fancy language and his expensive podiums
jokes.punchline: Mac thinks podiums are expensive status symbols
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Mac's working-class posturing includes bizarre focus on podium cost

jokes.text: Hey, y'all, it's me, Martina Martinez.
jokes.punchline: Dee adopts racist stereotypical persona
jokes.explanation: Callback to previous episodes but relies heavily on offensive stereotype for humor

jokes.text: Rape! Rape! Rape! My... This white boy's got his hands all over my beautiful brown body!
jokes.setup: Official trying to take microphone back
jokes.punchline: Dee escalates to false accusations in character
jokes.explanation: Escalation works for cringe factor but humor relies on offensive racial performance

jokes.text: When was the last time a white lady pick up after you when you threw your orange soda and your grape sodas on the ground?!
jokes.punchline: Specific mention of stereotypical beverages
jokes.explanation: Weak humor relying on racial stereotypes about beverage preferences - lazy writing

jokes.text: Look, it would've been fine if Dee hadn't incited a race war.
jokes.punchline: Mac casually blames Dee for starting a 'race war'
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery - treats massive social upheaval as minor inconvenience. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: We should make rugs for the bar. That's a good idea.
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to discussing bar rugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - their ability to completely move on from disasters to random new schemes

jokes.text: He's a twink versatile. You watch yourself. I wouldn't be able to satisfy him. A twink versatile? That's impressive.
jokes.punchline: Mac's continued expertise and Dennis's vanity concerns
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier gay taxonomy. Dennis's sexual insecurity is unexpected and funny.

jokes.text: Did you cut the brakes again? Yep. Why would you do that, Charlie? That's just what I do. Wild card, bitches!
jokes.punchline: Charlie proudly owns his destructive wild card identity
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and escalation - Charlie embraces the chaos he creates. 'Wild card, bitches!' is iconic delivery.

jokes.text: Try the brakes. Try the... Did you cut the brakes again? Yep. Charlie. Why would you do that, Charlie? That's just what I do. Wild card, bitches!
jokes.setup: Car trouble during their final attempt
jokes.punchline: Charlie's signature destructive behavior with catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to wild card theme. Charlie's casual admission and iconic catchphrase delivery.

jokes.text: So, the rugs? Yeah, the rugs. It's always been the rugs.
jokes.setup: Strike ending makes their scheme pointless
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to the rug scheme as if it was always the plan
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing their delusional ability to rewrite history - suddenly rugs were always the goal
